mappings.rationale: Covers prehistoric cultures and their practices including burial customs and ritual behavior, directly relevant to 821.A on prehistoric religion.
Covers archaeological methods for interpreting prehistoric beliefs and practices, relevant to 821.A.1 on methods of interpretation of prehistoric religion.
Covers anthropological study of primitive religion, including animism, totemism, shamanism, and rites of passage — core topics of 821.B.
Covers traditional religions of indigenous African peoples, directly relevant to 821.B.5.a on primitive religions of Africa.
Covers indigenous peoples of North America including their religious beliefs and practices, relevant to 821.B.5.c on religions of indigenous peoples of the Americas.
Addresses the nature and function of myth in primitive religion, directly relevant to 821.B.3 on myth and symbol in primitive religion.
Covers mythological traditions worldwide including those of indigenous and primitive peoples, relevant to 821.B.3 on the role of myth in primitive religion.
Covers magic as a religious and ritual practice, relevant to 821.A.2.f on sorcery and magic and 821.B.4.e on shamanism and divination.
Covers witchcraft beliefs and practices that have roots in prehistoric and primitive religion, relevant to 821.A.2.f on sorcery.
Covers pre-Christian and nature-based religious traditions, relevant to 821.B.4.b on worship of natural objects and 821.B.4.d on ancestor worship.
Covers druidic religion of the ancient Celts which preserves elements of prehistoric and primitive religious practice, relevant to the cultural overlap between prehistoric and primitive religion.
Covers ritual practices including sacrifice, purification, and passage rites, directly relevant to 821.B.4.a on primitive religious practices.
Covers Celtic peoples whose religious practices retained elements of prehistoric religion, relevant to understanding the transition from prehistoric to documented religion.
Covers the Ice Age period during which early prehistoric religious practices developed, providing context for 821.A.3 on religions of prehistoric cultural stages.
Covers human evolution and the development of symbolic and ritual behavior, providing context for the emergence of prehistoric religion in 821.A.
Covers ancient Near Eastern cultures that bridged prehistoric and early historical religion, providing context for 821.A.3 on religions attributed to various prehistoric stages.
Covers Aztec civilization whose religion retained elements of earlier indigenous American religious traditions, relevant to 821.B.5.c on religions of indigenous peoples of the Americas.
Examines superstitious beliefs that have roots in primitive religious views of the sacred and profane, relevant to 821.B.2 on primitive views of reality.
